div1.entryFree: Leash, a string or thong by which a greyhound is led: not following my l. unwillingly, Wint. IV, 4, 477. like a fawning greyhound in the l. Cor. I, 6, 38. Used in contempt of persons yoked together: I am sworn brother to a l. of drawers, and can call them all by their Christen names, as Tom, Dick, and Francis, H4A II, 4, 7 (three greyhounds making a leash).
